title: Eduversal Principal Coaching Cycle Framework v1.0
description: Structured monthly coaching cycle between an Eduversal HQ Director (mentor) and a partner-school Principal (coachee). Operates in two modes: (1) Year-1 induction-overlay coaching as part of the Principal Induction handbook (Charter NN3-compliant, formative-only, NN1-protected, journal NN2-protected); (2) Year-2+ post-induction coaching as ongoing professional development that feeds principal_appraisal_v1 F2 secondary evidence + competency_framework/leaders progression. Replaces the gap where Principal 360 framework + observation rubric + appraisal framework existed but the coaching engine that connects them did not have a structural spec.
design_principles: Coaching is monthly, not episodic — rhythm builds the relationship that makes hard conversations possible.
Sessions are structured (60-90 min, 5-stage agenda), not free-form — structure protects the time and ensures coverage.
Coaching is forward-looking — past performance is observed via the rubric; coaching focuses on what to do next.
Year-1 coaching is firewalled from appraisal (NN1) — induction-stage coaching produces development, never evaluation.
The principal owns the cycle — the Director is mentor, not evaluator; coachee initiates the agenda.
Coaching feeds, but does not constitute, the Principal Appraisal — F2 evidence comes from observations, not from coaching session content.

regulatory_alignment.title: Permendikbud 6/2018 — Penugasan Guru sebagai Kepala Sekolah
regulatory_alignment.relevance: Principal supervisi duty includes receiving structured coaching from network-level mentors.

regulatory_alignment.title: Permendikdasmen 7/2025
regulatory_alignment.relevance: Principal appointment regulation; mentor pairing during the first 12 months is part of structured induction.

regulatory_alignment.title: Permendiknas 13/2007
regulatory_alignment.relevance: Five competency dimensions — coaching focuses develop these dimensions.

framework_anchors.principal_induction_handbook: docs/induction/handbook-principal-v1.json — Year-1 coaching cycles map to the 4 induction windows (Listen, Diagnose, Act, Anchor)
framework_anchors.principal_observation_rubric: Academic Hub/resources/principal-observation-rubric.json — coaching focus areas reference the same 8 foci P1-P8 used in observation
framework_anchors.principal_appraisal_v1: Academic Hub/resources/principal-appraisal-framework-v1.json — coaching feeds F2 secondary evidence (observation primary), F5 self-assessment input
framework_anchors.principal_360_framework: docs/cross-module/principal-360-framework-v1.json — mid-year 360 results feed into coaching session design
framework_anchors.leaders_competency_framework: competency_framework/leaders — coaching session goals reference competency progression
framework_anchors.operating_cadence: Academic Hub/resources/principal-operating-cadence.json T-P5 tasks (staff development) — coaching reinforces the principal's own staff-development practice

year_1_induction_mode.mentor_role: HQ Director (Primary or Secondary, matching school stage range) — must hold active principal_mentor_endorsement certification (Charter NN3)
year_1_induction_mode.cadence: monthly — minimum 12 sessions across the induction year
year_1_induction_mode.session_purpose: Formative development. Each window's coaching focus aligns to the induction handbook's window-specific tasks. Charter NN1: NEVER feeds appraisal.
year_1_induction_mode.data_lineage: Stored in principal_coaching_sessions/{sessionId}; readable by mentor + coachee + central_admin only; explicitly NOT included in principal_annual_appraisals data_sources.
year_1_induction_mode.termination: First formal coaching cycle ends at induction completion (Year 1, week 40). Transition to year_2_plus_mode at start of Year 2.

year_2_plus_mode.mentor_role: HQ Director (continues from Year 1 by default; reassignment requires central_admin + Foundation Rep approval). Must hold active principal_mentor_endorsement.
year_2_plus_mode.cadence: monthly during Sem-1 + Sem-2 (10 sessions per academic year minimum); additional sessions on request
year_2_plus_mode.session_purpose: Ongoing professional development. Each session focuses on 1-2 principal_observation_rubric foci tied to the principal's IPDL goals + most recent observation findings + 360 results. Sessions feed F5 self-assessment input + may inform F2 observation focus areas.
year_2_plus_mode.data_lineage: Stored in principal_coaching_sessions/{sessionId}; visible to mentor + coachee + central_admin. Aggregate session count + cycle continuity feeds principal_annual_appraisals.sourcesUsed.coaching_continuity field. INDIVIDUAL session content NEVER feeds appraisal scoring (preserves coaching honesty).
year_2_plus_mode.termination: No automatic termination. Continues throughout principal's tenure. Mentor reassignment, coachee request to pause, or principal departure are the only termination paths.

agenda_stages.label: Personal & Operational Check-In
agenda_stages.purpose: Brief surface check on principal's wellbeing and operational state. Establishes presence; acknowledges that leadership is human work.
facilitator_questions: How are you doing as a person, not just as a principal, this month?
What single operational issue is taking the most space in your head right now?
agenda_stages.outputs: Mentor notes — confidential, never shared beyond mentor + coachee.

agenda_stages.label: Review of Last Session's Commitments
agenda_stages.purpose: Verify that commitments named in the previous session were enacted. Closes the accountability loop without making coaching feel like inspection.
facilitator_questions: Last month you committed to [X]. What happened?
If it didn't go as planned, what made it harder than expected?
agenda_stages.outputs: Commitment landing record (landed / partial / didn't land + reason). Tracked across sessions to identify patterns.

agenda_stages.label: Focus Topic Coaching
agenda_stages.purpose: Coachee-initiated topic — typically tied to the current induction window (Year 1) or current IPDL goal + recent observation findings (Year 2+). The Director uses GROW or Cognitive Coaching framework rather than direct advice.
agenda_stages.framework_used: GROW (Goal-Reality-Options-Will) for tactical coaching; Cognitive Coaching for reflective coaching
facilitator_questions: What is the goal you want to work toward in this conversation?
What is the current reality — what data, what evidence?
What options have you considered? What other options exist?
What will you commit to before our next session?
agenda_stages.outputs: Named coaching focus + named SMART commitment for next session.

agenda_stages.label: Strategic Horizon Conversation
agenda_stages.purpose: Step back from immediate tactical issues to the principal's 6-12 month horizon. Connects current decisions to longer-term direction. In Year 1, links to the induction window's strategic intent. In Year 2+, links to the principal's 3-year SIP goals + competency progression target.
facilitator_questions: Six months from now, when this work has played out, what will you wish you had paid more attention to?
Is there a leadership decision approaching that you want a thinking partner on?
agenda_stages.outputs: Strategic horizon notes — preserved in coaching log for continuity across sessions.

agenda_stages.label: Close & Logging
agenda_stages.purpose: Confirm the named commitment, log the session metadata, schedule the next session.
agenda_stages.outputs: Session record written to principal_coaching_sessions; commitment + horizon notes captured; next session date confirmed.

session_structure._note: 60-minute version compresses stages 4 and 5 to 10 minutes total. 90-minute version (extended) is used quarterly for deeper strategic conversations.

session_focus_areas_year_1._note: Year-1 coaching focuses align to the induction window the principal is currently in. The mentor uses this map to choose focus topics that reinforce induction handbook objectives.

stage_1_listen.coaching_emphasis: Listening discipline, school-state diagnosis without intervention, building trust before action
typical_focus_topics: How do I listen without rushing to fix?
What are the school's existing strengths I should preserve?
Who are the informal influencers in my school I need to understand?

stage_2_diagnose.coaching_emphasis: Evidence-based diagnosis, distinguishing structural vs personality issues, framing problems for action
typical_focus_topics: How do I separate structural problems from individual personality clashes?
What evidence am I missing to make a confident diagnosis?
Which problems are mine to solve and which belong to the team?

stage_3_act.coaching_emphasis: Sequencing change initiatives, building coalition before announcement, monitoring implementation
typical_focus_topics: Which initiative do I lead first, second, third?
Who must be on board before I announce this change?
How do I monitor whether the change is landing without it feeling like surveillance?

stage_4_anchor.coaching_emphasis: Embedding routines as institutional practice, succession-aware design, transition to Year 2 mode
typical_focus_topics: How do I make this routine survive my eventual departure?
What am I building that someone else could take over without my presence?
What transitions to Year 2 — what do I sustain, what do I pivot?

session_focus_areas_year_2_plus._note: Year-2+ coaching focus is driven by the principal's IPDL goals + most recent observation findings + most recent 360 results. The mentor uses this map to select rubric foci to anchor focus topics in.

P1_strategic_vision: When to revise the strategic plan vs hold the line
P2_instructional_leadership: Coaching the Subject Leaders who coach the teachers
Domain Heatmap analysis — what's the school's instructional weakness this quarter?
P3_managerial_operations: Time management as a principal — where is my time leaking?
Delegation decisions — what to keep vs let go
Operational response patterns — am I reactive or proactive?
P4_quality_assurance: QA cycles that produce action vs reports
Distinguishing surface metrics from leading indicators
P5_staff_development: Performance management conversations — when and how
Building Mid-career teachers into leadership pipeline
Wellbeing during difficult periods — staff and self
P6_stakeholder_engagement: External community partnerships — what to invest in
P7_compliance_safeguarding: Compliance posture — what's overdue, what's at risk
Safeguarding incident response — what worked, what to refine
Cambridge Centre standing — proactive vs reactive
P8_personal_conduct: Modelling growth in front of staff
Decision transparency when reasoning is complex

_charter_compliance.NN2_journal_separation: true (this is NOT the principal_journal — that is private to coachee only)
_charter_compliance.NN3_mentor_certification: verified at session creation — mentorUid must hold active principal_mentor_endorsement

feeds_into.principal_annual_appraisals_F5_self_asse: Aggregate session count + cycle continuity (number of sessions completed, average commitments-landing rate) — but NEVER individual session content. F5 evidence is the coachee's own annual reflection narrative, informed by but not derived from session records.
feeds_into.principal_annual_appraisals_F2_observati: Coaching session focus areas may suggest observation focus areas for the next scheduled visit — but only the observation data itself feeds F2. Coaching content does not.
feeds_into.principal_annual_appraisals_sourcesUsed: Aggregate metadata: 'coaching_continuity: 11/12 sessions completed' — proves cycle integrity, not session content.
feeds_into.competency_framework_leaders: Coachee's IPDL goals derived from coaching may target specific competency progression. Coaching session topics align to leaders track competencies.

does_NOT_feed.yayasan_reporting: Charter NN5 — yayasan-facing report is the appraisal triplet only. Coaching session content is never shared.
does_NOT_feed.school_appraisal_v2: Coaching content is mentor-coachee confidential — not part of the institution-as-system audit.
does_NOT_feed.induction_observations_or_journal_aggreg: Coaching is a separate confidential layer — not part of the induction analytics pipeline.

charter_compliance.NN1: Year-1 coaching sessions during induction NEVER feed into the principal's first formal appraisal at end of Year 2. Cycle integrity is the only metadata that crosses the boundary; individual session content does not.
charter_compliance.NN2: Principal_coaching_sessions is distinct from principal_journal (which is coachee-only-private). Coaching is a shared mentor-coachee record; journal is solo private reflection.
charter_compliance.NN3: Mentor pairing requires active principal_mentor_endorsement certification. Session creation rule reads mentor_certifications/{mentorUid}_principal_mentor_endorsement and rejects writes if not active.
charter_compliance.NN5: Yayasan never receives coaching content — only the aggregate appraisal triplet at year-end.

session_outcome_metrics_tracked._purpose: Track coaching cycle health without invading session content. These metrics aggregate behaviour, not content.

metrics.definition: Sessions completed / sessions scheduled across the academic year
Sessions where previous-month's commitment outcome marked 'landed' / total sessions reviewing a commitment
metrics.audited_in: Aggregate trend across sessions; not individual session content

metrics.definition: Same Director-mentor across 12+ months (proves relationship stability)
metrics.target: ≥1 year mentor stability except where mentor changes role
metrics.audited_in: Reassignment events logged in principal_coaching_sessions metadata

metrics.definition: Sessions of 60+ minutes / total scheduled sessions
metrics.target: ≥85% (some short sessions are normal; pattern of <60 min sessions signals time-boxing problem)

common_failure_modes.pattern: Sessions become operational problem-solving meetings rather than coaching conversations.
common_failure_modes.leadership_response: Mentor coaches herself: ask the principal a coaching question rather than answering with operational advice. Read Cognitive Coaching protocol mid-session if drift continues.

common_failure_modes.pattern: Principal consistently leaves session without naming a SMART commitment, or commitments are vague.
common_failure_modes.leadership_response: Mentor enforces specificity: 'What will you do, by when, with what?' If pattern persists across 2-3 sessions, surface the avoidance pattern explicitly.

common_failure_modes.pattern: Principal skips multiple sessions citing operational pressure.
common_failure_modes.leadership_response: Mentor reframes: 'The pressure is the reason for the session, not the obstacle to it.' If pattern persists, escalate to central_admin for cycle review (not punishment — to understand if mentor pairing or session timing needs adjustment).

common_failure_modes.pattern: Mentor uses session content to inform appraisal F2 evidence (Charter NN1 violation).
common_failure_modes.leadership_response: Audit: F2 data_source must reference principal_observations docs only, never principal_coaching_sessions docs. Discovery of violation triggers Director removal from mentor role + cycle integrity review for affected principal.

ui_to_build: AH /principal-coaching (coachee view: own session log + scheduled sessions + commitment history)
CH /principal-coaching-admin (mentor view: own mentees + cycle health metrics)
CH /principal-coaching-cycle-audit (central_admin audit view: cycle continuity across the network)
ui_implementation_phase_2.firestore_rules_to_add: principal_coaching_sessions — write requires mentor + active principal_mentor_endorsement; read scoped to mentor + coachee + central_admin only.
ui_implementation_phase_2.estimated_effort: 8-10 weeks of in-house dev (3 pages + rules + cycle audit dashboard)

provenance.regulatory_sources: Permendikbud 6/2018, Permendikdasmen 7/2025, Permendiknas 13/2007 — verbatim in docs/research/permendiknas/
provenance.framework_sources: Cambridge School Leader Standards 2023, GROW model (Whitmore 1992; later editions), Cognitive Coaching (Costa & Garmston, 4th ed 2016), Eduversal Principal Induction Charter
provenance.epdp_lineage: Authored as Round 7 closure of EPDP content layer. Companion to principal-360-framework-v1.json (mid-year + end-year data input), principal-observation-rubric.json (formative evidence), principal-appraisal-framework-v1.json (summative evaluation), handbook-principal-v1.json (induction structure).
provenance.audit_trigger: INDEX.md Phase-2 priority list 2026-05-08 — Principal Coaching cycle JSON spec was the only remaining content-layer spec across the EPDP suite. This file closes that gap.
